# Test basic mcpcat functionality
go build -o mcpcat .

# Create a test MCP trace file
cat > test.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05"}}
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}}}
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}}
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"tools":[]}}
EOF

# Test basic output
./mcpcat test.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv.*initialize'
stdout 'mcp-send.*protocolVersion'
stdout 'mcp-recv.*tools/list'
stdout 'mcp-send.*tools'

# Test colored output
./mcpcat -color test.mcp
stdout 'mcp-recv.*initialize'

# Test with non-existent file
! ./mcpcat nonexistent.mcp
stderr 'error.*nonexistent.mcp'

# Test reading from stdin
cat test.mcp | ./mcpcat
stdout 'mcp-recv.*initialize'
stdout 'mcp-send.*protocolVersion'

# Clean up
rm -f mcpcat test.mcp
